So, you know, recognizing that we're going to be publishing at a pace of probably four or five articles a week, you we've got to come up with a model that's scalable for us to review and to look at articles and make sure they're factually correct and aligned with what, you know, with what we think is, you know, you know, the right things to be saying in the market.
So what we're going to do is we're having conversations with a...
A team of, with two different teams, one on the customer success side of the house, another one on with Solution Architects.
And the idea will be is that we will create a GitHub board with, where we'll put each of the new articles as they come up.
We'll solicit volunteers from these two groups to do the review and to provide feedback and then move it forward to a status where it's approved for publishing.
So this will give us scalability and the ability to create multiple authors.
And then whoever the reviewer is will be the author and will be the byline.
So we'll solve a couple of problems by doing it this way.

But I don't think it's going to be a heavy lift.
And my expectation is that 90% of the...

But, but I think my, I prefer the approach of having humans read all of them and give a rubber stamp on them if they're good.
So I hope that doesn't put a wrench in what you guys are trying to do, but that's kind of what we want to do.

That reading the content before publishing.

So that's normal.
